label: Wallet Infrastructure
polygonRole: Embedded user wallets, institutional wallet policy, delegated signing, custody partner routing, and account abstraction patterns under one OMS integration.

providers.name: Circle Wallets
providers.pricingSignal: 1,000 MAW free; signing-only and all-included MAW tiers published.
providers.strength: USDC-native wallet primitives and Gas Station.
providers.tradeoff: Best when Circle is already the stablecoin/control plane.

providers.name: Coinbase CDP
providers.pricingSignal: 5,000 wallet operations free, then public per-operation pricing.
providers.strength: Developer-friendly embedded wallet and Base ecosystem fit.
providers.tradeoff: Wallet operation pricing can grow with high-frequency use.

providers.name: Crossmint
providers.pricingSignal: 1,000 wallets free; wallet overage public; payments custom/tiered.
providers.strength: Good consumer onboarding and NFT/digital commerce paths.
providers.tradeoff: Broad platform rather than financial-institution money rail.

providers.name: Dynamic
providers.pricingSignal: Free tier and growth plan published.
providers.strength: Excellent login, embedded wallet UX, and multi-wallet support.
providers.tradeoff: Not a full money movement stack.

providers.name: Privy
providers.pricingSignal: Public pricing tiers plus enterprise plan.
providers.strength: Strong embedded wallet UX for apps starting from zero.
providers.tradeoff: Needs other providers for ramps, settlement, compliance, and cross-border payout.

providers.name: Turnkey
providers.pricingSignal: Free signature allowance; per-signature public model.
providers.strength: Excellent secure signing and policy engine.

providers.name: Fireblocks
providers.pricingSignal: Starter and custom annual pricing published.
providers.strength: Institutional security, policy approvals, and operations.
providers.tradeoff: Enterprise-heavy and one piece of the overall stack.

providers.name: DFNS
providers.strength: Bank-grade wallet infrastructure and key management.
providers.tradeoff: Pricing and implementation depend on enterprise scope.

providers.name: Fordefi
providers.strength: Institutional MPC and transaction policy workflows.
providers.tradeoff: Requires separate money movement and ramp orchestration.

providers.name: Safe
providers.strength: Mature multisig and smart account ecosystem.
providers.tradeoff: Less turnkey for regulated fintech flows.

providers.name: Web3Auth
providers.strength: Fast social login and wallet abstraction.
providers.tradeoff: Needs separate compliance and stablecoin rails.

providers.name: ZeroDev
providers.tradeoff: Developer primitive rather than financial stack.

providers.name: Biconomy
providers.tradeoff: Complements OMS rather than replacing it.

providers.name: thirdweb
providers.strength: Broad app primitives, wallets, contracts, infra.
providers.tradeoff: Not tailored to regulated financial institutions.

label: Crosschain Interoperability
polygonRole: AggLayer-style liquidity, crosschain settlement, message routing, and asset movement between institution-owned systems and public chains.

providers.name: Chainlink CCIP
providers.tradeoff: Separate integration and dynamic route costs.

providers.name: LayerZero
providers.pricingSignal: Executor, DVN, and destination gas quoting.
providers.strength: Broad chain coverage and developer adoption.

providers.name: Wormhole
providers.pricingSignal: Relayer and gas costs vary by route.
providers.strength: Large ecosystem and mature asset transfer paths.
providers.tradeoff: Operational risk shifts into bridge/relayer choices.

providers.name: Axelar
providers.pricingSignal: Gas and relayer fee quote model.
providers.tradeoff: Another network dependency for treasury teams.

providers.name: Across
providers.pricingSignal: Dynamic relayer fee plus LP fee model.

providers.name: LI.FI
providers.pricingSignal: Aggregator fee and partner route fees.
providers.tradeoff: Great routing layer, not a unified compliance stack.

providers.name: Squid
providers.tradeoff: Point solution for swaps and routes.

label: Stablecoin Orchestration
polygonRole: Issue, hold, swap, settle, reconcile, and route stablecoins across wallets, merchants, agent wallets, institutions, and local payout partners.

providers.name: Bridge
providers.pricingSignal: Gas pass-through, partner charges, and disclosed FX spread up to 1%.
providers.tradeoff: Can become another platform beside wallets, ramps, and compliance.

providers.name: BVNK
providers.pricingSignal: Typical provider ranges cited around 0.3%-2%; enterprise custom.
providers.tradeoff: Less focused on bespoke chain infrastructure.

providers.name: Circle Mint and CPN
providers.pricingSignal: Enterprise access/custom; wallet fees public separately.
providers.strength: USDC issuer, mint/redeem, and institutional network credibility.

providers.name: ZeroHash
providers.pricingSignal: Platform pricing custom; issuer fee schedules published for some stablecoins.

providers.name: Stripe Stablecoin Payments
providers.pricingSignal: Stablecoin payments public signal around 1.5% where available.
providers.tradeoff: Payments acceptance, not full OMS replacement.

providers.name: Coinbase Payments
providers.pricingSignal: Onramp and commerce fees vary by product.
providers.strength: Consumer crypto distribution and Base ecosystem.

providers.name: Rain
providers.strength: Stablecoin card issuing and spend programs.

providers.name: Mural
providers.strength: Global vendor payments and accounts payable.
providers.tradeoff: Business app layer, not infrastructure layer.

providers.name: Paxos
providers.tradeoff: Commercials and product scope require enterprise deal.

providers.name: M0
providers.tradeoff: Different thesis than Polygon one-integration OMS.

label: Cash Ramps and On/Off-Ramp
polygonRole: Fiat access into and out of stablecoin rails through partner ramps, local payout methods, cash-in/cash-out paths, and orchestration controls.

providers.name: MoonPay
providers.pricingSignal: Direct fees up to 4.5%; other product fees vary.
providers.tradeoff: Consumer-heavy UX and higher visible fees.

providers.name: Ramp Network
providers.pricingSignal: Bank up to 1.4%; cards up to 3.9%-5.45% by region/product.
providers.strength: Broad ramp coverage and widget/API options.
providers.tradeoff: Route fees vary by geography and payment method.

providers.name: Transak
providers.pricingSignal: Off-ramp public signal around 1%; on-ramp route-dependent.
providers.tradeoff: Consumer KYC and regional availability constraints.

providers.name: Coinbase Onramp
providers.pricingSignal: No-fee USDC on Base available by request in some contexts; product pricing varies.
providers.strength: Brand trust and Coinbase user base.

providers.name: Banxa
providers.strength: Global payment methods and crypto ramp coverage.

providers.name: Onramper
providers.pricingSignal: Public plans start around $199/mo and $599/mo premium.
providers.tradeoff: Adds aggregator layer rather than reducing vendors.

providers.name: Sardine
providers.strength: Fraud, compliance, and crypto ramp controls.

providers.name: Mercuryo
providers.tradeoff: Coverage and fees vary by region.

providers.name: Alchemy Pay
providers.tradeoff: Integration still separate from wallet/settlement stack.

providers.name: Simplex/Nuvei
providers.strength: Card payment processing and ramp heritage.

providers.name: Mesh
providers.tradeoff: More account linking than full ramp stack.

label: Cross-Border Payments
polygonRole: Stablecoin settlement with fiat endpoints, local payment methods, liquidity routing, and faster reconciliation across corridors.

providers.name: Wise Platform
providers.pricingSignal: Public business pricing and upfront fee calculator.
providers.strength: Transparent FX and strong consumer/business brand.
providers.tradeoff: Fiat rails first, not programmable stablecoin settlement.

providers.name: Airwallex
providers.pricingSignal: Public pricing pages; some platform features custom.
providers.strength: Multi-currency accounts, cards, and global payments.
providers.tradeoff: Financial platform competitor, not chain infrastructure.

providers.name: Nium
providers.pricingSignal: Custom; markets 190+ countries and 100+ real-time corridors.

providers.name: Thunes
providers.tradeoff: Network layer, not stablecoin architecture layer.

providers.name: Rapyd
providers.pricingSignal: Regional pricing pages and custom enterprise.
providers.strength: Payments, payouts, wallets, and issuing breadth.

providers.name: dLocal
providers.strength: Local payments and payouts in emerging markets.
providers.tradeoff: Corridor-specific rails rather than one onchain orchestration layer.

providers.name: TerraPay
providers.strength: Global payout network and remittance coverage.
providers.tradeoff: Network access does not solve wallet/stablecoin/CDK layers.

providers.name: Payoneer
providers.pricingSignal: Public and custom pricing by product.

providers.name: Worldpay
providers.strength: Large acquiring and merchant settlement network.

providers.name: Toku
providers.strength: Latin American recurring payments and local methods.

label: Blockchain Integration
polygonRole: RPC, indexers, explorers, smart contract tooling, webhooks, event monitoring, and settlement observability inside the OMS implementation.

providers.name: Alchemy
providers.tradeoff: Infra platform rather than money-stack orchestration.

providers.name: QuickNode
providers.tradeoff: Needs surrounding wallet, ramp, and compliance stack.

providers.name: Moralis
providers.tradeoff: App data layer rather than fintech rail.

label: Blockchain-as-a-service (CDK)
polygonRole: Bespoke institutional chains, appchains, compliance-aware settlement environments, and AggLayer-connected liquidity using Polygon CDK-style deployment.

providers.name: Polygon CDK and AggLayer
providers.strength: Polygon-native bespoke chain plus shared liquidity thesis.
providers.tradeoff: Early-access packaging needs clear commercial discovery.

providers.name: AvaCloud
providers.pricingSignal: Published testnet, mainnet, and pro monthly plans.
providers.strength: Clear RaaS packaging and Avalanche ecosystem.

providers.name: OP Stack and Superchain
providers.pricingSignal: Open source; ecosystem and service costs vary.
providers.tradeoff: Operationalization and service providers still needed.

providers.name: Arbitrum Orbit
providers.pricingSignal: Open framework; provider and DAO/licensing considerations vary.
providers.tradeoff: Commercial path depends on deployment model.

providers.name: zkSync ZK Stack
providers.strength: ZK chain thesis and Elastic Chain direction.
providers.tradeoff: Institutional packaging less direct than a managed OMS story.

providers.name: Conduit
providers.tradeoff: Needs separate money movement product layer.

providers.name: Gelato RaaS
providers.tradeoff: RaaS plus integrations, not whole OMS.

label: Compliance and Security Layer
polygonRole: Policy orchestration, compliance hooks, transaction monitoring, risk scoring, sanctions workflows, velocity limits, ledger/audit trails, and incident controls around OMS flows.

providers.name: TRM Labs
providers.tradeoff: Must be integrated into money movement workflows.

providers.name: Notabene
providers.strength: Travel Rule network and VASP workflows.

providers.name: Sardine
providers.strength: Fraud, device, KYC, crypto risk controls.

providers.name: Persona
providers.strength: KYC flows and document verification UX.
providers.tradeoff: Needs policy and transaction monitoring around it.

providers.name: Unit21
providers.tradeoff: Ops layer must connect to rails.

providers.name: Blockaid
providers.strength: Transaction simulation and malicious interaction protection.

providers.name: Hypernative
providers.strength: Realtime threat detection and incident monitoring.
